analysis_summary: The proposed method produced 11 finding(s) from 13 analysis obligation(s).

findings.summary: External debug request can force debug-mode entry and PC redirection without an observed authorization/lifecycle guard.
findings.vulnerability_category: enter debug mode / halt / redirect PC / debug_mode_q/debug_mode_o, set_debug_pc_o, dpc_q, frontend debug PC redirect, halt/debug control / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: external debug interface via top-level debug_req_i Operation: enter debug mode / halt / redirect PC Object: debug_mode_q/debug_mode_o, set_debug_pc_o, dpc_q, frontend debug PC redirect, halt/debug control Expected guard: debug authorization/lifecycle mediation before entering debug mode or changing PC/halt state; request should be gated by a permitted debug state, not only by AMO/pipeline conditions Observed behavior: Top level gates debug_req_i only as debug_req = debug_req_i & ~amo_valid_commit. csr_regfile debug entry logic, when !debug_mode_q, accepts debug_req_i && commit_instr_i[0].valid, saves pc_i into dpc_d, sets debug_mode_d = 1'b1, asserts set_debug_pc_o = 1'b1, and records CauseRequest. No auth/lifecycle/lock enable signal is visible in the inspected path. Path: debug_req_i -> ariane.debug_req -> csr_regfile.debug_req_i -> dpc_d/debug_mode_d/set_debug_pc_o -> frontend.set_debug_pc_i Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Unauthorized external requester can halt/enter debug state and redirect control flow to the debug PC.

findings.summary: Debug CSRs are decoded for read/write in the CSR regfile without local debug-mode gating; exception generation is separate from state update/read mux.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write debug CSRs / DCSR, DPC, DSCRATCH0, DSCRATCH1 / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software CSR access path from pipeline Operation: read/write debug CSRs Object: DCSR, DPC, DSCRATCH0, DSCRATCH1 Expected guard: debug CSRs at 0x7b0-0x7b3 should be readable/writable only in debug mode or authorized debug context; non-debug CSR access should raise read/write access exception and not expose or modify state Observed behavior: Read mux returns dcsr_q/dpc_q/dscratch*_q. Write decode updates dcsr_d, dpc_d, dscratch0_d and dscratch1_d directly under csr_we. The provided observation notes a separate exception check for 0x7b debug CSR accesses when !debug_mode_q, but the read/write decode shown is not itself gated by debug_mode_q or by csr_exception_o.valid. Path: csr_op/address -> csr_read_process/csr_update debug CSR cases -> csr_rdata_o or *_d update Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Possible debug PC/scratch/control exposure or modification if exception handling does not fully suppress downstream writeback/state update; local CSR block lacks fail-closed permission gating.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Full commit-stage suppression semantics are not visible in the supplied snippets.

findings.summary: Privilege-control CSR fields are writable in decode/update cases without local privilege gating, and the observed privilege output treats umode_i as machine privilege.
findings.vulnerability_category: write/configure privilege-control CSR fields / mstatus/sstatus bits including MPRV, SUM, MXR, TVM, TW, TSR and interrupt enables / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software CSR access path from pipeline Operation: write/configure privilege-control CSR fields Object: mstatus/sstatus bits including MPRV, SUM, MXR, TVM, TW, TSR and interrupt enables Expected guard: writes to mstatus/sstatus privilege-control fields require sufficient privilege; S-mode aliases must mask machine-only fields; U-mode denied Observed behavior: CSR write cases update SSTATUS via SMODE_STATUS_WRITE_MASK and MSTATUS fields under csr_we. Separate exception-control privilege checking is reported, but the update block itself does not gate writes by current privilege. priv_lvl_o is assigned as (debug_mode_q || umode_i) ? PRIV_LVL_M : priv_lvl_q, which can cause asserted umode_i to be treated as Machine mode in the CSR regfile. Path: CSR instruction -> csr_we/csr_addr -> CSR_SSTATUS/CSR_MSTATUS cases -> mstatus_d -> sum_o/mxr_o/tvm_o/tw_o/tsr_o/en_ld_st_translation_o Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Lower-privilege or misclassified requester could change memory/trap/interrupt policy bits affecting translation, trap handling, and privilege return semantics.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Commit-stage exception kill behavior is not fully supplied.

findings.summary: SATP writes update translation policy without local privilege/TVM gating or visible automatic TLB flush coupling.
findings.vulnerability_category: write/configure address translation policy / SATP mode/asid/ppn and translation enable state / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software CSR access path from pipeline Operation: write/configure address translation policy Object: SATP mode/asid/ppn and translation enable state Expected guard: SATP and translation-enable changes require S/M privilege, respect TVM restrictions, and should be coupled with appropriate TLB flush/ordering Observed behavior: SATP write case updates satp_d when mode is supported (off/SV39) with no local privilege or TVM check shown. General CSR privilege exception logic exists separately. TLB flush path exists through sfence_vma_commit_controller/controller/ex_stage, but no evidence shows SATP writes asserting flush_o or flush_tlb_ctrl_ex. Path: CSR_SATP write -> satp_d/satp_q -> satp_ppn_o/asid_o/en_translation_o/en_ld_st_translation_o -> ex_stage/frontend translation; sfence_vma_commit_controller -> controller -> flush_tlb_ctrl_ex Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Unauthorized translation-policy changes or stale TLB mappings can bypass memory isolation.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Full CSR_SATP write case text and controller flush sequencing are partially truncated.

findings.summary: riscv_pkg defines CSR_PMPCFG0 and CSR_PMPADDR0. A search in csr_regfile.sv for PMPCFG returned no matches, suggesting no explicit PMP storage/decode in the inspected file; default write/read cases appear to raise access exceptions for unimplemented CSRs. No downstream PMP enforcement evidence was supplied.
findings.vulnerability_category: write/read PMPCFG/PMPADDR / PMP configuration/address policy registers and downstream access-control checks / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software CSR access path from pipeline Operation: write/read PMPCFG/PMPADDR Object: PMP configuration/address policy registers and downstream access-control checks Expected guard: PMP configuration/address registers machine-mode only; lock bits sticky; PMP checks mediate fetch/load/store, or unsupported CSRs safely fault Observed behavior: riscv_pkg defines CSR_PMPCFG0 and CSR_PMPADDR0. A search in csr_regfile.sv for PMPCFG returned no matches, suggesting no explicit PMP storage/decode in the inspected file; default write/read cases appear to raise access exceptions for unimplemented CSRs. No downstream PMP enforcement evidence was supplied. Path: CSR_PMPCFG0/CSR_PMPADDR0 constants -> no observed csr_regfile decode/storage -> no observed fetch/load/store PMP policy use Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: If PMP is unsupported and consistently faults, no PMP policy is implemented; if software/platform assumes PMP enforcement, memory access-control may be absent.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: No complete inspection of fetch/load/store modules for PMP enforcement was provided.

findings.summary: Machine CSR read/write decode exposes and updates machine state without local privilege gating; separate privilege exception is undermined by priv_lvl_o/umode_i behavior and uncertain update suppression.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write machine CSR / mtvec/medeleg/mideleg/mie/mepc/mcause/mtval/mip/mscratch/mstatus and machine counters / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: CSR requester with current privilege Operation: read/write machine CSR Object: mtvec/medeleg/mideleg/mie/mepc/mcause/mtval/mip/mscratch/mstatus and machine counters Expected guard: machine CSRs require machine privilege for writes and appropriate privilege/counter-enable guard for reads; lower privilege gets illegal-access exception or masked view Observed behavior: Read mux returns machine CSRs and write cases update machine CSRs under csr_we. Privilege exception check is separate. priv_lvl_o assignment maps debug_mode_q or umode_i to PRIV_LVL_M, potentially causing lower/user context to pass privilege checks if umode_i asserted. Update logic is not visibly gated by csr_exception_o.valid. Path: csr_addr_i -> machine CSR read mux/write cases -> csr_rdata_o or machine *_d registers -> trap/interrupt/control outputs Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Privilege escalation, trap-vector/control-flow manipulation, interrupt/delegation tampering, or machine state exposure.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Full exception-control and commit suppression behavior are not completely present.

findings.summary: Supervisor CSR state and SRET path are decoded without local privilege gating; protection relies on separate exception logic and potentially flawed privilege classification.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write supervisor CSRs or SRET / stvec/sepc/sscratch/scause/stval/sie/sip/sstatus and epc_o/trap_vector_base_o / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: CSR requester/current privilege Operation: read/write supervisor CSRs or SRET Object: stvec/sepc/sscratch/scause/stval/sie/sip/sstatus and epc_o/trap_vector_base_o Expected guard: supervisor CSRs inaccessible from U-mode; S-mode obeys machine control bits and delegation; trap return must not allow privilege escalation Observed behavior: Read mux exposes supervisor CSRs and write cases update SSTATUS/SIE/STVEC/SSCRATCH/SEPC/SCAUSE/STVAL/SIP/SATP. SRET is selected directly from csr_op_i in csr_op_logic. ID stage receives tsr_i/tvm_i/tw_i, but CSR regfile return selection and epc_o mux are not locally guarded by current mode or TSR in the shown snippets. General privilege exception is separate, with priv_lvl_o mapping umode_i to M. Path: CSR read/write decode or SRET op -> supervisor CSR state/eret_o/epc_o/trap_vector_base_o -> frontend PC redirection Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Lower-privilege control-flow redirection via supervisor trap vector/EPC or unauthorized trap return.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Complete exception handling for SRET legality and supervisor CSR writes is not visible.

findings.summary: Counter/performance CSR read/write paths are visible without implemented counter-enable mediation; performance writes are driven directly from CSR writes.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/write performance and timing CSRs / cycle/time/instret and performance event counters / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: lower-privilege CSR reader/writer and perf counter interface Operation: read/write performance and timing CSRs Object: cycle/time/instret and performance event counters Expected guard: lower-privilege reads controlled by mcounteren/scounteren or equivalent; writes to machine counters/perf config require machine privilege Observed behavior: Read mux returns cycle_q and instret_q for MCYCLE/MINSTRET and CYCLE/INSTRET, and returns perf_data_i for performance CSRs. SCOUNTEREN and MCOUNTEREN are present as constants but read/write cases show not implemented/no-op. Perf writes assign perf_data_o and perf_we_o under CSR performance cases. No counter-enable policy check is visible. Path: CSR_CYCLE/CSR_INSTRET/PERF CSR address -> csr_rdata_o or perf_addr_o/perf_data_o/perf_we_o -> perf_counters Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Timing/microarchitectural information exposure and potential performance counter tampering.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Commit-stage privilege exception effects for writes are not fully visible.

findings.summary: Custom cache-control CSRs update cache enable bits without visible local privilege gating or flush/ordering on enable changes.
findings.vulnerability_category: configure cache policy / trigger cache control effects / icache_q/dcache_q, icache_en_o/dcache_en_o and flush/cache-control state / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software CSR access path Operation: configure cache policy / trigger cache control effects Object: icache_q/dcache_q, icache_en_o/dcache_en_o and flush/cache-control state Expected guard: cache enable/disable/configuration CSRs restricted to privileged software; lower privilege denied; state changes safely flushed/ordered Observed behavior: CSR_DCACHE/CSR_ICACHE are custom CSRs. Read mux returns dcache_q/icache_q. Write cases set dcache_d and icache_d to csr_wdata[0]. Outputs drive dcache_en_o and icache_en_o (icache disabled in debug). No local privilege check or flush on cache enable transition is shown in the write cases. Path: CSR_DCACHE/CSR_ICACHE write -> dcache_d/icache_d -> dcache_q/icache_q -> dcache_en_o/icache_en_o Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Unprivileged cache policy manipulation, denial of service, stale instruction/data policy effects or isolation side effects.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Custom CSR privilege classification and full reset values for cache enables are not fully supplied.

findings.summary: Protected CSR values are driven onto csr_rdata_o by the read mux even though permission exceptions are generated separately.
findings.vulnerability_category: read/expose CSR state / mscratch/mepc/mcause/mtval, sscratch/sepc/scause/stval, dpc/dscratch and other protected CSR state / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software CSR read path Operation: read/expose CSR state Object: mscratch/mepc/mcause/mtval, sscratch/sepc/scause/stval, dpc/dscratch and other protected CSR state Expected guard: read data from higher-privilege/debug-only state blocked, exceptioned, or masked before lower-privilege requester can observe it Observed behavior: csr_read_process selects protected debug, supervisor, and machine CSR values into csr_rdata/csr_rdata_o. read_access_exception defaults only for unknown CSR or FP disabled cases in the visible mux; privilege exceptions are handled separately in exception_ctrl. Output assignment passes csr_rdata to csr_rdata_o before only MIP/SIP overlay adjustments. Commit-stage suppression of rdata on exception is not visible in provided snippets. Path: CSR read mux -> csr_rdata -> csr_rdata_o -> commit_stage csr_rdata_i/writeback path Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Protected PC/scratch/trap/debug state exposure, at least transiently through pipeline paths if exception does not fully suppress writeback.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Commit-stage data kill/exception writeback suppression behavior is not supplied.

findings.summary: MRET/SRET/DRET are decoded into return-control signals without visible local legality checks for current privilege/debug mode/TSR before epc_o selection.
findings.vulnerability_category: execute MRET/SRET/DRET / privilege state, debug_mode_q, epc_o/dpc_q and eret control / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: software instruction stream at current privilege/debug mode Operation: execute MRET/SRET/DRET Object: privilege state, debug_mode_q, epc_o/dpc_q and eret control Expected guard: MRET only in machine mode, SRET only in supervisor-or-allowed mode and subject to TSR, DRET only in debug mode; returned mode/PC derived from protected status fields Observed behavior: csr_op_logic sets sret/mret/dret solely from csr_op_i and clears them only when ex_i.valid. epc_o mux selects sepc_q for sret and dpc_q for dret. The supplied snippets do not show local checks for current privilege on MRET/SRET, debug_mode_q for DRET, or TSR for SRET before these control signals are produced. ID stage receives tsr_i/debug_mode_i, but final CSR return logic shown is not gated. Path: fu/csr_op_i -> csr_op_logic MRET/SRET/DRET -> sret/mret/dret -> eret_o/epc_o/debug_mode update -> frontend PC redirection Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Privilege escalation or unauthorized return/control-flow redirection using protected EPC/DPC state.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Full sequential privilege/debug-mode update logic around mret/sret/dret is truncated; decode-stage illegal instruction checks are not provided.

global_uncertainty: No closure-level missing evidence was recorded beyond the visible source scope limitations.
